root_text.an5.52:1.1: “Akusalarāsīti, bhikkhave, vadamāno pañca nīvaraṇe sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.an5.52:1.2: Kevalo hāyaṁ, bhikkhave, akusalarāsi yadidaṁ pañca nīvaraṇā.
root_text.an5.52:1.5: Akusalarāsīti, bhikkhave, vadamāno ime pañca nīvaraṇe sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.an5.52:1.6: Kevalo hāyaṁ, bhikkhave, akusalarāsi yadidaṁ pañca nīvaraṇā”ti.

translation_text.an5.52:1.1: “Mendicants, rightly speaking, you’d call the five hindrances a ‘heap of the unskillful’.
translation_text.an5.52:1.2: For these five hindrances are entirely a heap of the unskillful.
translation_text.an5.52:1.4: The hindrances of sensual desire, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.an5.52:1.5: Rightly speaking, you’d call these five hindrances a ‘heap of the unskillful’.
translation_text.an5.52:1.6: For these five hindrances are entirely a heap of the unskillful.”

variant_text.an5.52:1.2: hāyaṁ → hayaṁ (bj, pts1ed); cāyaṁ (sya-all, km); sāyaṁ (mr)
